Multinomial Sampling Test (MuST) in C++

Source: R/RcppExports.R
must_cpp.Rd

Inverse-CDF multinomial draw of a final state per cell (Mazy 2022, Appendix 3.B). This is the same test the reference clumpy implementation calls the "generalized allocation rejection test" (GART); the thesis itself only uses the MuST name, so we follow that here. NaN and negative probabilities are clamped to 0 (matching the reference clumpy Python).

Usage

must_cpp(P, states, u = NULL)

Arguments

P

Numeric matrix (n_cells x n_states); each row should sum to ~1 (include the "stay" column).

states

Integer vector of length ncol(P) giving the state id of each column.

u

Optional NumericVector of length nrow(P) of uniform draws in [0, 1) to replay instead of drawing from R's RNG. Lets an external uniform stream (e.g. numpy's, from the reference clumpy) be replayed for an exact cross-tool comparison of the pivot test.

Value

Integer vector of length nrow(P) with the sampled state per cell.

On this page
